name: get_available_slots
description: Look up real appointment slots for ONE day. One call per day_query. NEVER invent times or weekday names. day_query for tool calls only: 'tomorrow', 'day after tomorrow', or 'two days after tomorrow' (use next_day_query from the previous response — never guess). time_period: morning, afternoon, evening, or any (with time_preference). Read spoken_offer for slot times (e.g. 'Monday June 15 — 8 AM or 10 AM'). When moving to the next day, read next_day_ask to the customer (weekday name, e.g. 'Okay no worries — how about Thursday — would morning, afternoon, or evening work better for you?') — never say 'day after tomorrow'. If has_any_slots is false, call again with next_day_query. If scheduling_window_exhausted is true, use handoff script. For book_slot, use exact start from slots.

query_params_schema.description: Internal day key for the API only — not spoken to the customer. Start with 'tomorrow'. When moving days, use next_day_query from the previous response. Max 3 days.
morning, afternoon, or evening when the customer picks a block. Use 'any' only when calling with a custom time_preference.

Customer's preferred time frame AFTER they reject the first two slots. Pass their exact wording: 'around 2', 'between 10 and 12', 'after 3 PM', 'before noon', 'early afternoon', etc. Leave empty when first offering morning/afternoon/evening slots.
